props.title: Atlas Studio
props.description: A complete Builder page composed from editable Mantine-backed components.

css.background: radial-gradient(circle at 18% 18%, rgba(232,79,147,0.44), transparent 31%), radial-gradient(circle at 86% 8%, rgba(255,210,229,0.22), transparent 28%), linear-gradient(135deg, #2b2227 0%, #46353c 54%, #21191d 100%)

props.children: Design polished pages while every edit stays structured
Atlas Studio is a complete Builder page made from Mantine-backed components. Select a layer, edit props through RJSF, then tune CSS rules in the style tab.
Cards, typography, data tables and form fields expose different schema shapes, so the inspector has useful controls.
A beautiful page that still uses dynamic component config
The visual quality comes from Builder style objects and theme tokens, while props, data, actions and layer editing remain generated and inspectable.
Select headings, buttons and cards to edit strings, enums, booleans and arrays from registry schemas.
Apply presets or individual CSS rules to selected nodes without changing their component props.
Metrics, tables and buttons include data/action metadata so the inspector has meaningful content.
A dashboard area for testing nested layers, selected styles and table props.

props.title: Editable page queue
props.description: Rows are static here, while the node keeps a sourceId for the data tab.

props.children: Brief a page without leaving the builder
This form uses multiple field schemas so the properties inspector shows realistic inputs, selects and textarea controls.

props.name: urgent
props.label: Flag this as a launch-critical page

props.children: Use this page as the visual smoke test
Open the example, select layers, edit props, switch to style/data, and confirm the GrapeJS-inspired panels stay functional.

aiHints.intent: Provide a polished page for testing the Builder visual editor and property panels.
aiHints.audience: CyberVinci Builder users validating component editing
aiHints.context: Use this file to inspect the GrapeJS-inspired panel skin and RJSF property editing.
